project.title: Anti-proteolytic regulation of KRAS by USP9X/NDRG3 in KRAS-driven cancer development
project.projectDescription: Cancers with activating mutations of KRAS show a high prevalence and poor prognosis but remain intractable, requiring innovative strategies to overcome the poor targetability of KRAS. Here, we report that KRAS expression was post-translationally up-regulated through the deubiquitination of KRAS protein when the scaffolding function of NDRG3 (N-Myc downstream regulated gene 3) promoted specific interaction between KRAS and a deubiquitinating enzyme, USP9X. In KRAS-mutant pancreatic or lung cancer cells KRAS protein expression, downstream signaling, and cell growth were highly dependent on NDRG3. In conditional KrasG12D knock-in mouse models of pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma (PDAC), Ndrg3 depletion abolished Kras protein expression in pancreas, and suppressed pancreatic intraepithelial neoplasia (PanIN) formation. Mechanistically, KRAS protein bound to the C-terminal serine/threonine-rich region of NDRG3, subsequently going through the deubiquitination by USP9X recruited to the complex. This interaction could be disrupted in a dominant-negative manner by a C-terminal NDRG3 fragment that can bind KRAS but is defective in USP9X binding, highly suppressing the KRAS protein expression and KRAS-driven cell growth. In summary, KRAS-driven cancer development critically depends on the deubiquitination of KRAS protein mediated by USP9X/NDRG3, and KRAS-addicted cancers could be effectively targeted by inhibiting the KRAS-NDRG3 interaction.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: IP eluted proteins are digested using Strap micro (PROTIFI, product no. C02-micro-80) following manufacturer’s protocol v4.7. 1μg trypsin/lysC (promega, part no. V5073) was added to each samples and incubated overnight at 37 ℃ . pepides were dried using speedvac. RSLCnano u3000 / Orbitrap Exploris 240 (Thermo) system was used for analysis. Each sample was dissolved with 0.1% FA/ 2% Acetonitirle and peptides from 1ug protein amount were loaded onto the trap column(acclaim pepmap 100, 75 μm x 2cm, C18, 3 μm, PN 164535). Peptides were separated by analytical column (Double nanoViper pepmap RSLC C18, 75 μm x 50cm, 2 μm, 100Å, PN ES903) The column temperature was set to 60 degree. The mobile phases were 0.1% formic acid in water(buffer A) and 0.1% formic acid in acetonitrile(buffer B). The following gradient was used at a flow rate of 300nl/min : 2% B to 20% B in 100 min, 20% B to 32% B in 20min.  The survey scan settings are as follows: Resolution = 90,000, AGC 300%, mass range 350-1200 Th. The selected precursor was fragmented by HCD and analyzed by orbitrap. Other parameters for MS/MS scan are as follows: Top15 double play, Resolution= 30,000, max IT=200ms, AGC standard, Threshold 1E5, normalized collision energy = 30%, isolation width = 2, dynamic exclusion parameter exclude after n times = 1, exclusion duration time = 20sec, mass tolerance low/high = 10ppm.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: aw data from LCMSMS were analyzed with maxquant v2.4.0.0 and perseus v2.0.6.0. Maxquant parameters were as follow :　database = uniprot Homo sapiens, enzyme = trypsin/P, variable modification = Oxidation(M), Acetyl(protein N-term), fixed modification = carbamidomethyl (C) and match between runs.
keywords: Kras protein deubiquitination; ndrg3; usp9x; pancreatic cancer

references.referenceLine: Koo H, Park KC, Sohn HA, Kang M, Kim DJ, Park ZY, Park S, Min SH, Park SH, You YM, Han Y, Kim BK, Lee CH, Kim YS, Chung SJ, Yeom YI, Lee DC. Anti-proteolytic regulation of KRAS by USP9X/NDRG3 in KRAS-driven cancer development. Nat Commun. 2025 16(1):628

identifiedPTMStrings.name: No PTMs are included in the dataset

Characteristics[Organism]: Human lung cancer cell lines (NCI-H520, NCI-H1975, PANC-1, HCC827, A549, NCI-H2009), human pancreatic cancer cell lines (BxPC-3, MIA PaCa-2, AsPC-1) and HEK293T cell line were purchased from American Type Culture Collection (ATCC).
Characteristics[CleavageAgent]: 1 μg trypsin/lysC (Promega, part no. V5073) was added to each sample and the reaction was incubated overnight at 37 °C.
Comment[EnrichmentMethod]: immunoprecipitated with pre-washed FLAG M2® affinity gel (Sigma-Aldrich)
Comment[AcquisitionMethod]: MS survey scan across 450–2000 m/z was acquired in data-dependent mode and positive ion mode
Comment[GradientTime]: 2% B to 20% B in 100 min, 20% B to 32% B in 20 min.
Comment[FragmentMassTolerance]: precursor and fragment mass tolerance were set to 3.0 Da and 1.0 Da, respectively.
Comment[NumberOfMissedCleavages]: a maximum of two missed cleavages.
Comment[Separation]: The mobile phases were 0.1% formic acid in water (buffer A) and 0.1% formic acid in acetonitrile (buffer B).
Comment[SpecilizedMS]: Raw data from LC-MS/MS were analyzed with maxquant v2.4.0.0 and perseus v2.0.6.0 software.

Characteristics[CleavageAgent]: 1 μg trypsin/lysC (Promega, part no. V5073) was added to each sample and the reaction was incubated overnight at 37 °C.
